content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master`, read the changed br health/process code and the affected milestone mutation paths, and found two fix-now correctness gaps.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: The new beads health guard only runs inside `handle_propose_new_bead`; the normal bead-closing and bead-claiming flows still mutate `.beads` without any preflight validation.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2998-3029
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:204-216
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:377-430
- src/cli/project.rs:964-981
remediation_hint: Move the health check into a shared helper and invoke it before every milestone mutation entrypoint that updates or closes bead state, not just before proposed-bead creation, so conflicted or missing `.beads/issues.jsonl` stops the operation before `br update`/`br close`/`br sync` runs.
   Rationale: This bead is supposed to prevent mutations on corrupted bead state, but the common success-reconciliation and claim paths still bypass the new guard entirely. A repo with merge markers or a missing export can still be mutated through the normal workflow.
2. finding_summary: `check_beads_health()` misidentifies ordinary `issues.jsonl` content as a merge conflict because it uses raw substring checks for `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, and `>>>>>>>`, and it also collapses every non-`NotFound` read failure into `ConflictMarkers`.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:25-31
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:45-46
remediation_hint: Detect real conflict-marker lines instead of any substring match, and preserve other read failures as distinct errors so operators are not told to resolve a nonexistent merge conflict.
   Rationale: Bead descriptions/comments are arbitrary text serialized into JSON strings, so valid content containing `=======` or similar tokens will now block all mutations with a false conflict error. The same code also turns permission/encoding/read failures into the wrong operator guidance.
